content.body:
## User Story

As a repo maintainer, I want the GitHub PR reviewer to be model- and provider-switchable via repo variables, so that I can default to Qwen3.6 Plus via OpenRouter — and fall back to Anthropic Sonnet or any other OpenRouter model — without editing the workflow file.

## Context

`.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml:48` hardcodes `--model claude-sonnet-4-6` and keys the reviewer to `secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` on line 26. The enable-gate variable `vars.CLAUDE_REVIEW` (line 12) also implies a specific vendor. The project already has a provider-switchable pattern for the diagnosis path (`AI_PROVIDER` + `AI_MODEL_FAST` + `AI_MODEL_STRONG`) used by `scripts/self-healing.py:293,322` and `playwright/typescript/utils/diagnosis.util.ts:26-31,234-235`, but the reviewer has never adopted it.

Approach: keep `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` in place and route it to OpenRouter by setting `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`; OpenRouter exposes an Anthropic-compatible `/v1/messages` endpoint. Introduce **separate** reviewer vars (`AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER`, `AI_REVIEW_MODEL`) — reusing the diagnosis vars would couple the two paths and silently break the reviewer when `AI_PROVIDER=gemini` (the action has no Gemini compat).

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** `vars.AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` is unset or `openrouter`, `secrets.OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is set, and a PR is opened
**When** the `claude-code-review.yml` workflow runs
**Then** the resolver step outputs `provider=openrouter`, `model=qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, `base_url=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`; the `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` step completes without auth errors; the reviewer posts a formal `gh pr review` verdict on the PR.

**Given** `vars.AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER=anthropic` and `secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is set
**When** a PR is opened
**Then** the resolver outputs `model=claude-sonnet-4-6`, `base_url=` (empty), the action uses the Anthropic key, and the reviewer posts a formal `gh pr review` verdict — identical behavior to today.

**Given** `vars.AI_REVIEW_MODEL=qwen/qwen3-coder` and `vars.AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER=openrouter`
**When** a PR is opened
**Then** the resolver outputs `model=qwen/qwen3-coder` and the action invokes that model specifically.

**Given** `vars.AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` is set to anything other than `openrouter` or `anthropic`
**When** the workflow runs
**Then** the resolver step fails loudly with `::error::Unknown AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER '<value>' (expected: openrouter | anthropic)` and the action step is not reached.

**Given** the merge lands before `CLAUDE_REVIEW` → `AI_REVIEW` is renamed in GitHub → Settings → Variables
**When** a PR is opened
**Then** the job is skipped (gate evaluates false) — caller must rename the variable in the GitHub UI as part of the rollout.

## Implementation Hint

Replace the steps block in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` so the provider/model/base-URL are resolved in a dedicated step and fed into the action:

```yaml
    if: github.actor != 'dependabot[bot]' && vars.AI_REVIEW == 'true'
    # ...
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Resolve reviewer provider settings
        id: reviewer
        env:
          AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER: ${{ vars.AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER }}
          AI_REVIEW_MODEL: ${{ vars.AI_REVIEW_MODEL }}
        run: |
          provider="${AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER:-openrouter}"
          case "$provider" in
            openrouter) default_model="qwen/qwen3.6-plus"; base_url="https://openrouter.ai/api/v1" ;;
            anthropic)  default_model="claude-sonnet-4-6"; base_url="" ;;
            *) echo "::error::Unknown AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER '$provider' (expected: openrouter | anthropic)"; exit 1 ;;
          esac
          model="${AI_REVIEW_MODEL:-$default_model}"
          { echo "provider=$provider"; echo "model=$model"; echo "base_url=$base_url"; } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: ${{ steps.reviewer.outputs.base_url }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ steps.reviewer.outputs.provider == 'openrouter' && secrets.OPENROUTER_API_KEY || secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          track_progress: true
          prompt: |
            # ...unchanged...
          claude_args: |
            --model ${{ steps.reviewer.outputs.model }} --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,Bash(gh pr review:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*)"
```

## Definition of Done

> **Design superseded by #220.** PR #218 delivered this DoD as specced, but follow-up issue #220 (bug: OpenRouter slug rejected pre-flight) revised the design — `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` / `AI_REVIEW_MODEL` were removed in favour of direct pass-through via `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` + `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` + `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL`. DoD items referencing the removed vars are left unchecked because the current repo no longer reflects them.

- [ ] ~~`.vars.example` renames `CLAUDE_REVIEW=` → `AI_REVIEW=` and documents `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER=` and `AI_REVIEW_MODEL=` with their defaults.~~ _(Rename done; the two new vars were introduced by #218 and then removed by #220.)_
- [x] `.env.example` documents `OPENROUTER_API_KEY=`.
- [ ] ~~`.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` gate uses `vars.AI_REVIEW`; resolver step branches on `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER`; action receives the right key, base URL, and model.~~ _(Gate on `vars.AI_REVIEW` — done. Resolver step deleted by #220.)_
- [ ] ~~`README.md` renames the `CLAUDE_REVIEW` catalog entry and table row to `AI_REVIEW`, adds rows for `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` and `AI_REVIEW_MODEL`, and mentions `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in the env-var prose.~~ _(Rename + `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` prose done; the two `AI_REVIEW_*` rows were added by #218 and then removed by #220.)_
- [x] `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is added as a GitHub Actions secret; `AI_REVIEW` is created in repo Variables (value `true`) and `CLAUDE_REVIEW` is deleted — done **before** merging the PR.
- [x] A test PR on the implementation branch is reviewed end-to-end by `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` via OpenRouter, producing a formal `gh pr review` verdict. _(Achieved after the #220/#221/#222/#224/#225 chain resolved the pre-flight + prompt-framing issues.)_
- [ ] ~~A second test PR with `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER=anthropic` is reviewed by `claude-sonnet-4-6` with identical-to-today behavior.~~ _(Var removed by #220; native-Anthropic path now selected by leaving `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` empty — not smoke-tested post-#220.)_
- [x] `grep -rn CLAUDE_REVIEW` across the repo (excluding `.claude/worktrees/`) returns no hits.
- [x] `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml'))"` exits 0.

## Summary

Closes #217. Replaces the hardcoded Claude Sonnet reviewer with a provider-switchable setup. Defaults to **Qwen3.6 Plus via OpenRouter** (Anthropic-compatible `/v1/messages` endpoint); flip `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER=anthropic` in repo Variables to revert.

- New repo variables: `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` (`openrouter` default | `anthropic`), `AI_REVIEW_MODEL` (optional model override per provider).
- Renames the enable-gate `CLAUDE_REVIEW` → `AI_REVIEW` so the name is vendor-neutral.
- Resolver step in `claude-code-review.yml` validates that the required secret is non-empty before the action runs — prevents the Anthropic key from being sent to `openrouter.ai` if `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` is missing.
- New secret requirement: `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` (documented in `.env.example` and `README.md`).

## Changes

- `.vars.example` — rename `CLAUDE_REVIEW` → `AI_REVIEW`; add `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER`, `AI_REVIEW_MODEL`.
- `.env.example` — add `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` placeholder.
- `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` — resolver step branches on provider, sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, picks the right secret, and passes the right `--model`.
- `README.md` — rename gate in catalog + table, add rows for the two new variables, document `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`.

## Rollout prerequisites (done before merge)

- `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` secret created
- `AI_REVIEW=true` repo variable created; `CLAUDE_REVIEW` deleted
- `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER=openrouter` and `AI_REVIEW_MODEL=qwen/qwen3.6-plus` set

## Test plan

> **Design superseded by #220.** The `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` path failed pre-flight (Claude Code SDK client-side slug validation) on the first live run. Follow-up #220 / #221 / #222 replaced `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` + `AI_REVIEW_MODEL` with direct `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` + `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_{SONNET,HAIKU}_MODEL` pass-through. Most of this test plan is moot — the vars it references no longer exist.

- [x] `grep -rn CLAUDE_REVIEW` (excluding `.claude/worktrees/`) returns no hits

## Assumptions flagged for verification

1. `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` forwards the `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` env var into the Claude Code runtime.
2. OpenRouter's `/v1/messages` endpoint accepts `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` (Anthropic-compatible routing).
3. Qwen via OpenRouter supports Anthropic-style tool use (required for `mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment` and `Bash(gh pr review:*)`).

If any of these fail during the Test plan checks, the fallback is a custom Python script (`scripts/pr-review.py`) following the `self-healing.py` dispatch pattern.

content.body:
## User Story

As a repo maintainer who enabled the provider-switchable PR reviewer from #217, I want the reviewer to actually work against OpenRouter/Qwen, so that PR reviews don't fail every run.

## Context

PR #218 merged a workflow that sets `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` and passes `--model qwen/qwen3.6-plus` to `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` (`.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml:88`).

On the first live run (PR #219, run [24405785979](https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24405785979/job/71288577136)) the action failed with:

> error: Claude Code returned an error result: **There's an issue with the selected model (qwen/qwen3.6-plus). It may not exist or you may not have access to it.** Run --model to pick a different model.

No traffic appeared in OpenRouter's logs — the SDK rejected the slug pre-flight.

Two root causes:

1. **Client-side slug validation.** Claude Code's SDK validates model slugs against an internal registry before any HTTP call. Non-Anthropic slugs like `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` are rejected pre-flight, so the override never takes effect.
2. **Wrong auth header.** OpenRouter's Anthropic-compat endpoint expects `Authorization: Bearer` (→ `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN`), not `x-api-key` (→ `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`). The current workflow passes the OpenRouter key via the `anthropic_api_key` action input, which sets `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` — wrong header.

Per [OpenRouter's Claude Code integration guide](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/coding-agents/claude-code-integration), the documented mechanism is `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=<key>` plus `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` (both tiers — Claude Code invokes Haiku internally for summarization).

### Design change

The fix is also a simplification: drop the translator layer. Today the workflow exposes `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` + `AI_REVIEW_MODEL` as user-facing vars and a resolver step translates them into SDK-level env vars. Replace that with **direct pass-through from repo Variables to the SDK env vars** — secrets in `.env` / GH Secrets, non-secrets in `.vars` / GH Variables, no translation layer.

| Layer | Name | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Secret | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Native Anthropic auth (x-api-key). Used when `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is empty. |
| Secret | `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | Proxy auth (Bearer). Used when `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is set. |
| Variable | `AI_REVIEW` | Enable gate (unchanged from #217). |
| Variable | `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` | Empty = native Anthropic. `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` = OpenRouter. |
| Variable | `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` | Sonnet-tier slug override. Required when base URL is set. |
| Variable | `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` | Haiku-tier slug override. Required when base URL is set. |

**Removed vars (migration step for maintainers):** `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER`, `AI_REVIEW_MODEL`.

**Auth-header convention in the workflow (the one remaining bit of logic):** base URL empty → `anthropic_api_key` input = `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret (x-api-key); base URL non-empty → `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` env = `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` secret (Bearer). The Anthropic key is structurally never transmitted to the proxy.

A pre-flight validation step aborts the job early if `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is set without both model overrides — otherwise Claude Code's default slugs (`claude-sonnet-4-6`, `claude-haiku-4-5`) would be sent to OpenRouter and either silently re-routed or 4xx'd.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL=qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, and `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` secret set
**When** a PR is opened
**Then** the action step's env contains `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`, `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN=<OpenRouter key>` (Bearer), `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL=qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, no `--model` flag is passed in `claude_args`, HTTP traffic is visible in OpenRouter's logs, and a formal `gh pr review` verdict is posted on the PR.

**Given** `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` unset (empty) and `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` secret set
**When** a PR is opened
**Then** the action uses Anthropic's native API via the `anthropic_api_key` input (x-api-key auth), `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` is empty, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` are empty (Claude Code uses `claude-sonnet-4-6` / `claude-haiku-4-5`), no `--model` flag — identical behavior to the pre-#217 reviewer.

**Given** `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is set but `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` or `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` is empty
**When** the workflow runs
**Then** a validation step fails loudly with `::error::ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is set, so both ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL and ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL must also be set` before the action step runs.

**Given** `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is set and `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` secret is missing
**When** the workflow runs
**Then** `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` is empty and the action step fails at auth time (401 from the proxy) — the Anthropic key is never transmitted to the proxy.

## Implementation Hint

Replace lines 12–88 of `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` with:

```yaml
    if: github.actor != 'dependabot[bot]' && vars.AI_REVIEW == 'true'
    timeout-minutes: 10
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      pull-requests: write
      id-token: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 1

      - name: Validate reviewer configuration
        env:
          BASE_URL: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL }}
          SONNET: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL }}
          HAIKU: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL }}
        run: |
          if [ -n "$BASE_URL" ] && { [ -z "$SONNET" ] || [ -z "$HAIKU" ]; }; then
            echo "::error::ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL is set, so both ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL and ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL must also be set. Claude Code's defaults (claude-sonnet-4-6, claude-haiku-4-5) don't exist on proxies like OpenRouter."
            exit 1
          fi

      - uses: anthropics/claude-code-action@v1
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL }}
          ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL != '' && secrets.OPENROUTER_API_KEY || '' }}
          ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL }}
          ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL }}
        with:
          anthropic_api_key: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL == '' && secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY || '' }}
          track_progress: true
          prompt: |
            # ...unchanged...
          claude_args: |
            --allowedTools "mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment,Bash(gh pr review:*),Bash(gh pr diff:*),Bash(gh pr view:*)"
```

Update `.vars.example` to drop `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER=` and `AI_REVIEW_MODEL=` and add `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL=`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL=`. Update `README.md` catalog and table rows correspondingly.

## Definition of Done

- [x] `--model` flag removed from `claude_args` in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml`.
- [x] Resolver step deleted. Validation step added — aborts on misconfigured proxy without model overrides.
- [x] Action step sets `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` via `env:`, sourced directly from `vars.*` / `secrets.*` with no intermediate step.
- [ ] `anthropic_api_key` input only passes a value when `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is empty — Anthropic key is structurally never sent to the proxy. _(Revised by follow-up #222: `anthropic_api_key` now also carries `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` when proxying, required to satisfy `claude-code-action@v1`'s own pre-flight validator. No-leak property preserved — Anthropic key still never sent to the proxy.)_
- [x] `.vars.example` drops `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` / `AI_REVIEW_MODEL`, adds `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` with inline comments explaining "empty = native Anthropic".
- [x] `README.md` variables catalog and table updated — `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` / `AI_REVIEW_MODEL` rows removed, the three new rows added.
- [x] GitHub repo Variables migrated: delete `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` and `AI_REVIEW_MODEL`; create `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL`.
- [x] End-to-end verified on a follow-up PR: OpenRouter logs show incoming request for the configured slug; action posts a `gh pr review` verdict.
- [x] `grep '\-\-model' .github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` returns no matches.
- [x] `grep -nE 'AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER|AI_REVIEW_MODEL' .` (excluding `.claude/worktrees/`) returns no matches.

content.body:
## Summary

Closes #220. Fixes the reviewer from #218, which failed pre-flight with `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` — Claude Code's SDK validates model slugs client-side and rejects non-Anthropic ones before any HTTP call reaches OpenRouter.

Two root causes, one combined fix:

1. **`--model` doesn't work for non-Anthropic slugs.** Replaced with the SDK's documented env-var override: `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` + `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` (Haiku also overridden because Claude Code invokes it internally for summarization).
2. **Wrong auth header.** OpenRouter expects `Authorization: Bearer` (→ `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` env). The old workflow passed the OpenRouter key via the `anthropic_api_key` action input, which sets `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (x-api-key). Now split: Bearer via env when proxying, x-api-key via input when native.

### Design change — direct pass-through

Per "`.env` for secrets, `.vars` for non-secret variables", the translator layer is gone. The workflow reads GH repo Variables straight through to Claude Code SDK env vars; the resolver shell step is deleted.

| Layer | Name | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| Secret | `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` | Native Anthropic auth (x-api-key). |
| Secret | `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` | Proxy auth (Bearer). |
| Variable | `AI_REVIEW` | Enable gate (unchanged). |
| Variable | `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` | Empty = native Anthropic. Set = proxy. |
| Variable | `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` | Sonnet-tier slug. Required when base URL is set. |
| Variable | `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` | Haiku-tier slug. Required when base URL is set. |

Removed: `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER`, `AI_REVIEW_MODEL` (both deleted from GH Variables as part of rollout).

Auth convention via inverse ternaries on `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`:
- Empty → `anthropic_api_key: secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, no Bearer.
- Non-empty → `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN: secrets.OPENROUTER_API_KEY` env, `anthropic_api_key: ''`.

A preflight validation step aborts the job loudly if `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is set without both model overrides — otherwise Claude Code's defaults (`claude-sonnet-4-6` / `claude-haiku-4-5`) would hit the proxy and either silently re-route or 4xx.

## Rollout prerequisites (done)

- `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` = `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1` set
- `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` = `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` set
- `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` = `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` set
- `AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER` and `AI_REVIEW_MODEL` deleted

## Test plan

- [x] Validation guard smoke-tested locally (`/tmp/validate_test.sh`) — 6/6 branches behave correctly: native (no models), native (sonnet-only override), proxy (both set), proxy (sonnet missing → error), proxy (haiku missing → error), proxy (both missing → error).
- [x] `/security-review` — zero findings. Cross-provider key leak traced impossible across all 4 (BASE_URL set/unset) × (secret present/empty) combinations for both ternaries.
- [x] `/simplify` — one fix applied (polarity-explaining comment above ternaries). Other suggestions (inline validation, README trim, hardcode defaults) triaged as judgment calls and skipped.
- [x] Workflow YAML parses (`yaml.safe_load`).
- [x] `git grep AI_REVIEW_PROVIDER\|AI_REVIEW_MODEL` outside `.claude/worktrees/` returns no matches.
- [x] After merge: the next incoming PR triggers the reviewer, OpenRouter logs show a request for `qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, and a formal `gh pr review` verdict is posted. _(Achieved on PR #219 after the #222/#224/#225 follow-up chain fixed the action pre-flight validator + prompt + verdict-framing issues — run 24412848507 succeeded with `APPROVED` verdict, commit a0041928.)_
- [x] After merge: manually flip `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to empty, confirm native Anthropic path still works with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. _(Verified 2026-04-15 on throwaway PR #231 — with all three `vars.ANTHROPIC_*` temporarily deleted, reviewer ran on native Anthropic via `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`, posted `APPROVED` verdict ending with `_Reviewed by native Anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6_`. Assumption above confirmed: empty env vars fall back to Claude Code's built-in defaults.)_

## Assumption

Claude Code SDK treats `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL=""` (empty env) as equivalent to unset — falling back to the built-in default. `self-healing.yml` already relies on this pattern for `AI_MODEL_FAST` / `AI_MODEL_STRONG` without issue, so the precedent is solid. If the native-Anthropic path breaks post-merge with an "empty model" error, the follow-up is to conditionally set explicit defaults (`claude-sonnet-4-6` / `claude-haiku-4-5`) in the workflow.

content.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to #221 (which closed #220). The reviewer still fails on PR #219's run with:

> Environment variable validation failed: Either ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN is required when using direct Anthropic API.

Root cause: `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` has its own pre-flight env validator — one level above the Claude Code SDK — that hard-requires `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (or `CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN`) to be non-empty. `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` alone (which the SDK accepts for Bearer auth) is not accepted by the action's validator. #221 passed `anthropic_api_key: ''` when proxying, tripping this validator.

**Fix:** pass the OpenRouter key via `anthropic_api_key` input when proxying, in addition to `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` env. The Anthropic SDK then sends both `x-api-key` and `Authorization: Bearer` headers with the same key; OpenRouter accepts Bearer and ignores (or also accepts) the x-api-key header.

Run that failed: https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24408010412 — env wiring was correct (`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, both tier-model vars, `ANTHROPIC_AUTH_TOKEN` all set), but `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ` was empty.

## Ternary safety

New `anthropic_api_key` input:

```yaml
anthropic_api_key: ${{ (vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL != '' && secrets.OPENROUTER_API_KEY) || (vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL == '' && secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) || '' }}
```

Walk-through of all four (BASE_URL set/empty) × (relevant-secret set/empty) cases:

| BASE_URL | Target secret | Result | Leak? |
|---|---|---|---|
| set (proxy) | OPENROUTER_API_KEY set | OPENROUTER_API_KEY | ✅ no |
| set (proxy) | OPENROUTER_API_KEY empty | `''` | ✅ no — empty, not Anthropic key |
| empty (native) | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY set | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY | ✅ no |
| empty (native) | ANTHROPIC_API_KEY empty | `''` | ✅ no |

Cross-provider leak is structurally impossible: when `BASE_URL != ''`, the second `&&` evaluates to `false` and the ANTHROPIC_API_KEY branch is unreachable.

## Test plan

- [x] YAML parses (`yaml.safe_load`).
- [x] After merge → merge main into PR #219 again → reviewer runs, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (in env, via input) is now non-empty, action's pre-flight passes, OpenRouter logs show a request for `qwen/qwen3.6-plus`, Qwen posts a `gh pr review` verdict. _(Verdict posted on PR #219 — first as `COMMENTED` (commit 944cccfb, run 24412334990), then as `APPROVED` after #225 tightened the verdict framing (commit a0041928, run 24412848507). Pre-flight passed.)_
- [x] Native Anthropic path unchanged: flip `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to empty in repo Variables, confirm reviewer still works with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. _(Verified 2026-04-15 on throwaway PR #231 — with all three `vars.ANTHROPIC_*` temporarily deleted, `anthropic_api_key: secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` branch of the ternary was exercised, pre-flight passed, reviewer posted `APPROVED` verdict via native Anthropic path.)_

content.body:
## User Story

As a repo maintainer using a non-Anthropic model (Qwen on OpenRouter) as the PR reviewer, I want the reviewer prompt to frame the work as an autonomous agentic task, so that the model reliably executes the review and posts a formal `gh pr review` verdict instead of responding conversationally.

## Context

`.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml:28-45` defines the `prompt:` passed to `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`. It starts with *"Please review this pull request"* and places the mandatory `gh pr review` action 10+ lines down, after a list of focus areas. For Anthropic's own Claude models this works — they interpret the context and execute. For other instruction-tuned models routed via OpenRouter it fails in two distinct ways:

- **`qwen/qwen3-coder-plus`** (run [24411572539](https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24411572539)): responded once with *"I'll analyze this and get back to you."* and stopped. `num_turns: 1`, `total_cost_usd: $0.25`, no tool calls. Read the prompt as a conversational opener and never initiated the autonomous work.
- **`qwen/qwen3-coder`** (run [24411178668](https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24411178668)): ran 20 turns, produced review content — but fabricated 2 false issues ("`quality-metrics` not registered in `.mcp.json`" and "setup instructions don't include `npm run build`"), both of which contradict the actual diff. No `gh pr review` invoked.

Root cause: polite framing reads as advisory to non-Claude models, and the cluttered prompt doesn't visibly signal "autonomous task with a mandatory final action." A sharper prompt — imperative framing, mandatory action first, explicit anti-conversational + anti-hallucination constraints — should help all supported models, not just Qwen.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` points to OpenRouter and `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` is any Qwen coder model
**When** a PR is opened
**Then** the reviewer completes in more than 1 turn, does not respond with a generic acknowledgment message, and terminates by invoking `gh pr review <number>` exactly once (`--approve`, `--request-changes`, or `--comment`).

**Given** the review body is fetched via `gh api repos/:owner/:repo/pulls/<number>/reviews`
**When** the reviewer completes
**Then** every concrete issue the review claims must be verifiable against the PR's actual diff (no hallucinated file paths, no "missing" content that is actually present).

**Given** `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is empty (native Anthropic, Claude Sonnet path)
**When** a PR is opened
**Then** the reviewer continues to work as before — the sharpened prompt should not regress Claude's behaviour.

**Given** a reviewer has just completed
**When** the review body is measured
**Then** its length is <= 2000 characters (enforced by prompt instruction).

## Implementation Hint

Replace the `prompt:` block in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` (currently lines 28-45 after the Task-#220 changes). Move the mandatory final action to the top and frame the whole prompt as an imperative autonomous task:

```yaml
          prompt: |
            You are executing a code review of pull request #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}
            in ${{ github.repository }}. This is an autonomous task: complete it using tool calls
            only. Do not reply in prose. Do not say "I will analyze". Do not output review text
            as a message.

            MANDATORY final action — invoke `gh pr review ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}`
            exactly once, with one of:
              - `--approve -b "..."`          if the PR is acceptable as-is
              - `--request-changes -b "..."`  if changes are required
              - `--comment -b "..."`          if you have observations but no verdict

            Task completion is measured by whether that call succeeds. If the final
            `gh pr review` is not invoked, the task has failed.

            Steps:
              1. Fetch the diff with `gh pr diff ${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}`.
              2. Read the diff and identify issues against these criteria:
                 - Playwright test correctness (selectors, assertions, fixture usage)
                 - Page Object Model conventions (extending AbstractPage, static
                   url/title/accessKey)
                 - TypeScript quality (types, imports, path aliases)
                 - Potential bugs or flaky test patterns
                 - Consistency with existing patterns in the codebase
              3. (Optional) attach line-anchored comments via
                 `mcp__github_inline_comment__create_inline_comment`.
              4. Call `gh pr review` per the mandate above. The review body must summarise
                 findings and must be under 2000 characters. Every claim must be verifiable
                 in the diff — do not invent issues.
```

## Definition of Done

- [x] `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` prompt rewritten with imperative framing, mandatory `gh pr review` action first, anti-conversational + anti-hallucination constraints, and 2000-char body cap.
- [x] YAML still parses (`yaml.safe_load`).
- [x] Post-merge verification PR with Qwen as the reviewer: `num_turns > 1`, a formal `gh pr review` row appears in `gh pr view <n> --json reviews`, no fabricated findings against the diff, review body <= 2000 chars. _(Run [24412334990](https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24412334990) with qwen3-coder-plus — 18 turns, clean body, no hallucinations. Verdict was `COMMENTED` with an "As an AI…" disclaimer — later addressed by follow-up #225 tightening the verdict guidance.)_
- [x] Post-merge verification PR with Anthropic native as the reviewer (flip `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to empty on one branch): behaviour identical to pre-change (no regression). _(Verified 2026-04-15 on throwaway PR #231 with all three vars (`ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL`, `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL`) temporarily deleted. `Report reviewer configuration` logged `provider=native Anthropic base_url=<native> sonnet=<default> haiku=<default>`; reviewer posted `APPROVED` verdict ending with `_Reviewed by native Anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6_`. Body 352 chars, clean markdown. Vars restored after.)_
- [x] No other workflow changes in the PR — scope limited to the prompt.

content.body:
## Summary

Closes #223. Rewrites the reviewer `prompt:` in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` to frame the work as an autonomous agentic task instead of a polite advisory request. Two distinct failure modes observed with Qwen models on PR #219 motivated this:

- **`qwen/qwen3-coder-plus`** (run [24411572539](https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24411572539)): 1 turn, no tool calls, responded *"I'll analyze this and get back to you."* and stopped.
- **`qwen/qwen3-coder`** (run [24411178668](https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24411178668)): 20 turns, hallucinated 2 false issues, no `gh pr review` verdict.

Root cause: *"Please review this pull request"* reads as conversational to non-Claude models, and the mandatory `gh pr review` action was buried 10+ lines into the prompt.

## Changes

Prompt rewrite moves the mandatory final action to the top and adds explicit guardrails:

- **Line 1:** "This is an autonomous task: complete it using tool calls only. Do not reply in prose."
- **Line 2 block:** `MANDATORY final action — invoke gh pr review ... exactly once` (the one thing the reviewer must do).
- **Line 3:** "Task completion is measured by whether that call succeeds. If the final `gh pr review` is not invoked, the task has failed."
- **Numbered steps follow**, with the 4th step reiterating the `gh pr review` mandate and adding: "The review body must be under 2000 characters. Every claim must be verifiable in the diff — do not invent issues."

Scope limited to the prompt block — no other workflow changes.

## Test plan

- [x] `yaml.safe_load` — parses cleanly, 3 steps preserved.
- [x] Prompt length 1498 chars (under the 2000-char model output cap this request instructs).
- [x] **Post-merge**: re-run the reviewer on PR #219 (branch `feature/145`) with `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` — confirm `num_turns > 1`, a formal `gh pr review` verdict appears in `gh pr view 219 --json reviews`, and the review body contains no fabricated findings. _(Run 24412334990 — 18 turns, `COMMENTED` verdict, clean body. Verdict state was later tightened by #225.)_
- [x] **Post-merge**: flip `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to empty on a test branch — confirm native Anthropic path still behaves as before (no regression). _(Verified 2026-04-15 on throwaway PR #231 — native-Anthropic reviewer posted a 352-char `APPROVED` verdict on `claude-sonnet-4-6` with the sharpened prompt, cleanest review in the cross-model comparison.)_
- [x] **Post-merge**: `grep 'Please review' .github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` returns no matches.

## Known limitation

Same workflow-tamper guard as #218/#221/#222 — `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` refuses to exchange OIDC for an app token when the workflow file on the PR branch differs from `main`. The reviewer's own check on this PR will fail with *"Workflow validation failed…"* and that's expected. Real verification happens on the first PR after merge.

## Summary

Follow-up to #224 (closed #223). The sharpened prompt now produces real reviews, but qwen3-coder-plus consistently picks `--comment` over `--approve` — hedging with *"As an AI, I cannot approve this PR"*. `COMMENTED` reviews don't satisfy the `check-approval` gate in `playwright-typescript.yml:94` or `bruno.yml:38` (both filter for `.state == "APPROVED"`), so the heavier CI jobs skip.

## Changes

Tightens the MANDATORY-verdict block in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` to push toward a binary `--approve` / `--request-changes` decision:

- **`--approve` marked as the default.** *"Minor nits do not block approval."*
- **`--request-changes` scoped to "ONLY when the PR has a concrete blocking issue."** Stops Qwen from retreating there to avoid approving.
- **`--comment` scoped to "drafts / WIP PRs or PRs where the diff is structurally unreviewable (mass renames, generated files). Do not use as a fallback."**
- **Explicit anti-hedge:** *"You are the reviewer; produce a verdict. Do not add disclaimers like 'As an AI, I cannot approve' — your role is to evaluate code quality against the criteria, not defer to human judgment."*

## Test plan

- [x] YAML parses (`yaml.safe_load`). Prompt length now 2055 chars.
- [x] **Post-merge**: re-run reviewer on PR #219 with `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus`. Expect `.reviews[].state` to be `APPROVED` (or `CHANGES_REQUESTED` if Qwen flags something real), not `COMMENTED`. `check-approval` job should then unlock downstream CI. _(Run 24412848507 on commit a0041928 — verdict `APPROVED`. `check-approval` on PR #219 concluded SUCCESS.)_
- [x] No "As an AI" disclaimer in the review body. _(`gh pr view 219 --json reviews` — neither review body contains the disclaimer.)_
- [x] **Post-merge**: flip `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to empty, confirm native Anthropic path unaffected by the prompt change. _(Verified 2026-04-15 on throwaway PR #231 — native Anthropic reviewer produced an `APPROVED` verdict, no "As an AI" hedge. Binary-verdict prompt changes from this PR work identically on the native path.)_

## Previous run for comparison

- Run [24412334990](https://github.com/hubertgajewski/orwellstat/actions/runs/24412334990) — qwen3-coder-plus with #223 prompt — posted `COMMENTED` review with "As an AI, I cannot approve this PR" disclaimer. Technically successful (18 turns, $1.19, clean body, no hallucinations) but the wrong verdict state for CI purposes.

## Summary

Docs-only cleanup on `.vars.example` — three issues found while operating the reviewer:

1. **Wrong OpenRouter URL in the comment.** The example said `https://openrouter.ai/api/v1`; correct value is `https://openrouter.ai/api` (the Anthropic SDK appends `/v1/messages` internally, so `/api/v1` produces a double-`v1` 404). Added a note explaining why.
2. **Stale model examples** pointed to `qwen/qwen3.6-plus` (lines 26 and 30). Replaced with empirically-probed working examples.
3. **No guidance on avoiding thinking-mode models.** Added a one-line warning plus two working examples per tier (`openai/gpt-5.3-chat`, `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` for sonnet; `openai/gpt-4o-mini`, `qwen/qwen3-coder-flash` for haiku).

No functional changes — `.vars.example` is a template, not runtime code.

## User Story As a tester investigating a failed run with `AI_DIAGNOSIS=true`, I want the diagnosis fixture to log which provider and which fast/strong model it actually used, so that I can verify that `AI_MODEL_FAST` / `AI_MODEL_STRONG` overrides took effect without resorting to a throwaway `console.log` in the source. ## Context `playwright/typescript/utils/diagnosis.util.ts:215-270` attaches an AI-generated diagnosis when `AI_DIAGNOSIS=true`. The model selection at lines 233-236 merges env-var overrides with per-provider defaults from `MODEL_MAP` (lines 26-31), but the choice is never surfaced anywhere — the Playwright report attachment shows the diagnosis *text* but not which model produced it. Verifying override behavior during the #213 smoke required a throwaway `console.log('[ai-diagnosis-smoke] …')` line that was reverted immediately after. Making that line permanent gives every future diagnosis-enabled failure the same visibility for free, with zero cost on green runs (the existing `AI_DIAGNOSIS !== 'true'` / invalid-provider / missing-key guards short-circuit before the log). ## Acceptance Criteria **Given** `AI_DIAGNOSIS=true`, a valid `AI_PROVIDER`, and a matching API key secret, **When** any test fails, **Then** stdout contains exactly one `[AI diagnosis] provider=

fast= strong=` line for that failure, with the values matching the `models` object at `diagnosis.util.ts:233-236`. **Given** `AI_MODEL_FAST=gemini-2.5-flash-lite` and `AI_MODEL_STRONG=gemini-2.5-flash`, **When** a test fails with `AI_PROVIDER=gemini`, **Then** the logged values are `fast=gemini-2.5-flash-lite strong=gemini-2.5-flash`, not the `MODEL_MAP` defaults. **Given** `AI_DIAGNOSIS` is unset (or any test passes), **When** the fixture runs, **Then** no `[AI diagnosis]` line appears — the early-return at line 220 still short-circuits. ## Implementation Hint Insert one line in `attachAiDiagnosis` right after the `models` object is built: ```typescript const models = { fast: process.env.AI_MODEL_FAST || MODEL_MAP[provider].fast, strong: process.env.AI_MODEL_STRONG || MODEL_MAP[provider].strong, }; console.log(`[AI diagnosis] provider=${provider} fast=${models.fast} strong=${models.strong}`); ``` The `[AI diagnosis]` prefix matches the existing `console.warn('[AI diagnosis] skipped: …')` style at lines 251 and 268. ## Definition of Done - [ ] `playwright/typescript/utils/diagnosis.util.ts` logs `[AI diagnosis] provider=

fast= strong=` after the `models` object is resolved. - [ ] Log only fires when the existing `AI_DIAGNOSIS`, provider, and API-key guards all pass (unchanged gating). - [ ] Manual smoke: a failing test locally with `AI_DIAGNOSIS=true AI_PROVIDER=gemini AI_MODEL_FAST=gemini-2.5-flash-lite AI_MODEL_STRONG=gemini-2.5-flash` prints the override values verbatim. - [ ] `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. - [ ] `npm run format:check` passes.

content.body:
## User Story

As a repo maintainer verifying which configuration the PR reviewer actually ran with, I want the workflow to surface the provider (native Anthropic vs proxy) and both model slugs both in the Actions run log and as a trailing signature on the posted review body, so that confirming config changes (e.g. flipping `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`) doesn't require grepping action internals or inferring from HTTP traces.

## Context

`.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` routes the reviewer through either native Anthropic or an Anthropic-compatible proxy based on `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`, with `vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` / `vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` overriding the built-in defaults. None of these values are surfaced to the run log or the posted review. The verification chain for #217 / #220 / #221 / #222 / #223 / #224 / #225 repeatedly required cross-referencing run IDs with `gh api /pulls/:n/reviews` output and inferring the active path from request-level behaviour. The pending "native-Anthropic path regression" DoD item on #221 / #222 / #223 / #224 / #225 is unchecked specifically because there is no clean signal on the PR that a given review came from the native path.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** a PR is opened and the reviewer workflow runs,
**When** the job completes (success or failure),
**Then** the Actions run log contains a single line in the form `provider=<native Anthropic|proxy> base_url=<url-or-<native>> sonnet=<slug-or-<default>> haiku=<slug-or-<default>>` from a trailing step with `if: always()`.

**Given** the reviewer posts a `gh pr review` verdict,
**When** the review body is fetched via `gh pr view <n> --json reviews`,
**Then** the final line of `body` reads exactly `_Reviewed by <provider>/<sonnet-model>_` with the values that were templated into the prompt at job-run time.

**Given** `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` is empty (native Anthropic path),
**When** the workflow runs,
**Then** the log line shows `provider=native Anthropic base_url=<native>` and the review body signature reads `_Reviewed by native Anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6_` (or whatever native default is in effect).

## Implementation Hint

**Part 1 — trailing `Report reviewer configuration` step** after the action, guarded `if: always()` so the record survives action failures:

```yaml
      - name: Report reviewer configuration
        if: always()
        env:
          BASE_URL: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL }}
          SONNET: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL }}
          HAIKU: ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL }}
        run: |
          if [ -z "$BASE_URL" ]; then
            provider="native Anthropic"; url="<native>"
          else
            provider="proxy"; url="$BASE_URL"
          fi
          echo "provider=$provider base_url=$url sonnet=${SONNET:-<default>} haiku=${HAIKU:-<default>}"
```

**Part 2 — signature line appended to the review body.** Add to the end of the existing `prompt:` block:

```yaml
            Append this as the final line of the review body, verbatim:
            `_Reviewed by ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL != '' && 'proxy' || 'native Anthropic' }}/${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }}_`
```

GitHub Actions templating resolves the provider/model strings at job-run time, so the model just echoes the fully-formed signature.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` has a trailing `Report reviewer configuration` step with `if: always()` echoing `provider / base_url / sonnet / haiku` to stdout.
- [ ] The reviewer `prompt:` instructs the model to append a `_Reviewed by <provider>/<sonnet-model>_` signature line as the final line of the review body.
- [ ] YAML still parses (`python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml'))"` exits 0).
- [ ] Post-merge: a follow-up PR's reviewer run has both the log line in the job output AND a `_Reviewed by …_` signature visible via `gh pr view <n> --json reviews`.
- [ ] Post-merge: flipping `vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL` to empty on a throwaway test branch produces `provider=native Anthropic` in the log and a `_Reviewed by native Anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6_` signature — satisfies the pending native-Anthropic regression DoD on #221, #222, #223, #224, #225.
- [ ] PR body notes the usual `claude-code-action@v1` workflow-tamper guard (reviewer's own check on the PR will fail; real verification happens on the first PR after merge) — same caveat as #224.

items.title: Smoke: native Anthropic reviewer regression test

content.body:
## User Story

As a repo maintainer reading a PR reviewer's posted verdict, I want the `_Reviewed by <provider>/<model>_` signature to render as an italic line separated from the review body by a blank line, so that the markdown is clean regardless of which AI model is backing the reviewer.

## Context

#228 / PR #230 added a signature-line instruction to the `prompt:` block in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml`:

```yaml
            Append this as the final line of the review body, verbatim:
            `_Reviewed by ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL != '' && 'proxy' || 'native Anthropic' }}/${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }}_`
```

Verified live on the smoke PR #231 against five different model configurations:

| Model | Signature rendering |
|---|---|
| native Anthropic / `claude-sonnet-4-6` | Clean — actual blank line before the italic signature |
| proxy / `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` | Clean |
| proxy / `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` + `qwen/qwen3-coder-flash` | Clean |
| proxy / `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` + `openai/gpt-4o-mini` | **Broken** — emits literal `\n\n` (two backslash-n) before the signature |
| proxy / `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` on both tiers | **Broken** — same literal `\n\n` glitch |

Example of the broken rendering (from the `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` run):

> …No functional code changes; no Playwright/POM/TypeScript concerns applicable.\n\n_Reviewed by proxy/openai/gpt-5.3-chat_

The Anthropic-family models interpret the instruction semantically (a blank line is a natural visual separator); non-Anthropic models take "verbatim" too literally and also emit the implied leading newline as an escaped escape sequence. Cosmetic, not functional — the signature still identifies the model — but it degrades on the exact models we use in production.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** the reviewer runs on any supported model configuration (native Anthropic, Anthropic via proxy, Qwen, OpenAI via OpenRouter),
**When** the `gh pr review` body is fetched via `gh pr view <n> --json reviews`,
**Then** the signature line renders as `_Reviewed by <provider>/<model>_` preceded by either a genuine blank line or appended directly after the last content paragraph — never with a literal `\n` / `\\n` escape sequence in the text.

**Given** the prompt is changed,
**When** the existing Anthropic path is exercised (e.g. the first PR opened after merge),
**Then** the signature is still present, still correctly templated with the provider + sonnet model, and does not regress in markdown quality.

## Implementation Hint

Two candidate fixes; pick the one that behaves best when smoke-tested against all five model combos from #231:

**Option 1 — worked example under a heading.** Replace the current three-line instruction with:

```yaml
            At the end of the review body, append one blank line followed by this exact line:

            _Reviewed by ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL != '' && 'proxy' || 'native Anthropic' }}/${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }}_
```

Drop the outer backticks and the word "verbatim" — the worked example itself is the template, and the explicit "one blank line followed by" should stop the literal-`\n` rendering.

**Option 2 — drop the separator, let the signature be flush.** Replace with:

```yaml
            End the review body with this line (no leading blank line needed):

            _Reviewed by ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL != '' && 'proxy' || 'native Anthropic' }}/${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }}_
```

Accept that the signature will sit against the last content paragraph. Cleaner instruction, slightly uglier render on the native-Anthropic path.

Option 1 is preferred — preserves the current render quality on Anthropic models while fixing the non-Anthropic path.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` prompt rewritten so the signature instruction does not produce literal `\n` / `\\n` on `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` or `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus`.
- [ ] YAML still parses (`python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml'))"` exits 0).
- [ ] Post-merge: a smoke iteration (empty-commit loop à la #231) over the same five model combos shows no `\n` / `\\n` escape in any review body. Log via `gh pr view <n> --json reviews -q '.reviews[].body' | tail`.
- [ ] PR body notes the usual `claude-code-action@v1` workflow-tamper caveat — the reviewer's own check on the PR will fail; real verification happens on the first PR after merge plus a follow-up smoke pass.

content.body:
## Summary

Rewords the reviewer's signature-line instruction in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` so the blank-line separator between review body and signature is expressed semantically ("append one blank line followed by this exact line") rather than embedded in the instruction as an implied literal. Eliminates the `\n\n` escape-sequence rendering seen on `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` and `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` (single-tier) during yesterday's five-model smoke on PR #231.

Closes #232.

## Before / after

**Before:**

```
Append this as the final line of the review body, verbatim:
`_Reviewed by …_`
```

**After:**

```
At the end of the review body, append one blank line followed by this exact line:

_Reviewed by …_
```

Two changes:
- Drop the outer backticks around the template (models were interpreting "verbatim" to include the surrounding backticks on some runs).
- Replace "Append this as the final line ... verbatim" with explicit "append one blank line followed by this exact line" — tells the model semantically what we want, rather than relying on the one-line-after-the-instruction layout being picked up as the newline source.

## Test plan

- [x] YAML parses (`python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml'))"` exits 0).
- [x] Diff scope limited to the signature-instruction block (3 lines inserted, 2 removed).
- [x] `/security-review` — no findings (prompt string only; no env/secret surface).
- [x] `/code-review` — all CI-workflow checks pass: `timeout-minutes` intact, actions pinned, no new secret refs, no new inputs.
- [x] Expected-failure pattern confirmed: `review` failed with workflow-tamper guard (same as #224 / #230); `check-approval` passed; `Report reviewer configuration` ran despite the failure and emitted `provider=proxy base_url=https://openrouter.ai/api sonnet=openai/gpt-5.3-chat haiku=openai/gpt-4o-mini` — `if: always()` guard intact.
- [ ] Post-merge smoke on a throwaway branch — iterate `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` across the five combos used on #231:
  - `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` + `openai/gpt-4o-mini` (the primary fix target)
  - `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` + `qwen/qwen3-coder-flash`
  - `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` on both tiers (the other fix target)
  - `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` + `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5` (regression check)
  - native Anthropic with all three `vars.ANTHROPIC_*` empty (regression check)
  For each, fetch the review body via `gh pr view <n> --json reviews -q '.reviews[-1].body'` and confirm no `\n` / `\\n` escape sequence in the output — the signature should appear on its own line after a genuine blank line. `grep -F '\n\n' <body>` must return no matches.

## Known limitation

Same `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` workflow-tamper caveat as #224 / #230: the reviewer step on this PR will fail with *"Workflow validation failed…"* because the branch's copy of `claude-code-review.yml` differs from `main`. Expected. Real verification happens on the post-merge smoke pass described above.

items.title: Smoke: #232 signature-fix verification sweep

content.body:
## User Story

As a repo maintainer reviewing a PR, I want each reviewer run to leave exactly one artefact on the PR — the formal `gh pr review` verdict — so the timeline isn't cluttered with duplicate "Claude finished …" comments and stale `I'll analyze this and get back to you.` placeholders that some models leave behind.

## Context

Today, every reviewer run in `claude-code-review.yml` posts **two** things to the PR:

1. A regular issue **comment** (`Claude finished @user's task in Ns — [View job]…`) — created automatically by `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` *before Claude even runs*, then Claude is supposed to update it with the review output. Created because the workflow sets `track_progress: true` (line 53).
2. A **`gh pr review`** verdict — created by Claude per the imperative prompt, with the actual review body.

The two posts contain the same review content. They diverge in failure modes:

| Model | Auto-comment behaviour | `gh pr review` behaviour |
|---|---|---|
| Native Anthropic / `claude-sonnet-4-6` | Updates with task-checklist + analysis | Posts verdict review with body |
| `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` via OpenRouter | Same | Same |
| `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` (split or single tier) | **Leaves `I'll analyze this and get back to you.` placeholder forever** | Posts verdict review |
| `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` | Same dead placeholder | Posts verdict review (with literal `\n\n` before signature) |
| `@preset/qwen3-6-no-thinking` | Same dead placeholder | Posts verdict review |

Empirical evidence: PR #234 (signature-fix smoke sweep, closed) accumulated **6 separate "Claude finished" comments**, four ending with the dead placeholder; only the two Anthropic-family runs updated their comment with real content. The auto-comment is a complete loss for non-Anthropic models, and even on Anthropic models it's a duplicate of the verdict review.

The `\n\n` literal-newline glitch in #232 is downstream of the same root cause: with `track_progress: true`, the action injects a `mcp__github_comment__update_claude_comment` tool plus instructions like *"PR CRITICAL: After reading files and forming your response, you MUST post it by calling mcp__github_comment__update_claude_comment"*. Weaker models construct their `gh pr review -b "..."` shell argument inconsistently because they're juggling two posting paths — pasting a literal `\n\n` instead of an actual newline in the bash quoting.

The action documents (action.yml) that `track_progress` defaults to `false`. Setting it explicitly to `true` was inherited from earlier reviewer iterations (#218 onwards) without the dual-post implications being recognised. Switching it to `false` puts the action into "agent mode" (per `src/modes/detector.ts`): no auto-comment, no `update_claude_comment` tool, no "PR CRITICAL" framing — Claude has exactly one job, the `gh pr review` call.

PR #233 attempted to fix #232 by rewording the signature instruction. It made things worse — `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` started dropping the signature entirely (not just the blank line), and `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` still emitted literal `\n\n`. Reverting that rewording in the same PR keeps the prompt simple and tests the dual-post hypothesis cleanly.

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** the reviewer workflow runs on a PR,
**When** the job completes (success or failure) and the model has called `gh pr review`,
**Then** the PR has exactly one new entry in the comments timeline (the formal review) and zero new entries from Claude in the issue/PR comments (`gh pr view <n> --json comments | jq '[.comments[] | select(.author.login == "claude")] | length'` does not increase).

**Given** the reviewer runs on `openai/gpt-5.3-chat`, `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus`, `qwen/qwen3-coder-flash`, `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` via OpenRouter, native Anthropic, and `@preset/qwen3-6-no-thinking`,
**When** the verdict review body is fetched via `gh pr view <n> --json reviews -q '.reviews[-1].body'`,
**Then** the body ends with the templated signature line (`_Reviewed by <provider>/<sonnet-model>_`) and contains no `\n` / `\\n` literal escape sequence (`grep -F '\n\n' body` returns no matches).

**Given** the workflow is unchanged on `main`,
**When** `python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml'))"` runs,
**Then** it exits 0.

## Implementation Hint

Two changes in `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml`, both small:

1. **Line 53** — change `track_progress: true` → `track_progress: false`. Disables the action's tag-mode (auto-comment + comment-update tool + "PR CRITICAL" framing). Documented in the action's own `action.yml` and `src/modes/detector.ts`.
2. **Lines 92–93** — revert PR #233's signature rewording back to:

```yaml
            Append this as the final line of the review body, verbatim:
            `_Reviewed by ${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL != '' && 'proxy' || 'native Anthropic' }}/${{ vars.ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL || 'claude-sonnet-4-6' }}_`
```

The pre-#233 wording had qwen-coder-plus correctly emitting the signature (with the `\n\n` artefact); the post-#233 wording made qwen drop the signature entirely. With `track_progress: false`, the dual-post pressure that produced the `\n\n` should be gone, so the simpler "verbatim" instruction works for everyone.

No other changes. The `Validate reviewer configuration` step, the `Report reviewer configuration` step, the auth/env wiring, and the rest of the prompt remain as-is.

Closes #232 as a side-effect (the `\n\n` is expected to disappear once the dual-post pipeline is removed). If `\n\n` persists for any model after merge, file a separate follow-up — but most likely it won't.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` line 53: `track_progress: false`.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml` lines 92–93: signature instruction restored to the pre-#233 single-line `Append this as the final line of the review body, verbatim: \`...\`` form.
- [ ] YAML still parses (`python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml'))"` exits 0).
- [ ] Post-merge smoke (5–6 model combos as in PR #234, throwaway draft PR with empty-commit loop): for each iteration, `gh pr view <n> --json comments -q '[.comments[] | select(.author.login == "claude")] | length'` does **not** grow with each new commit (no new "Claude finished" comments per run); each `gh pr view <n> --json reviews -q '.reviews[-1].body'` ends with the signature line and contains no `\n` escape sequence.
- [ ] PR body notes the usual `claude-code-action@v1` workflow-tamper caveat — reviewer's own check on the PR will fail, real verification happens on the post-merge smoke pass.

content.body:
## Summary

Two surgical edits to `.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml`:

1. **`track_progress: true` → `false`** (line 53). Switches `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` from "tag mode" to "agent mode" per the action's documented `track_progress` input. Stops the action from auto-creating an issue comment per reviewer run, stops it from injecting `mcp__github_comment__update_claude_comment`, and stops the "PR CRITICAL: you MUST post via comment" framing into Claude's prompt. Result: each reviewer run posts exactly one artefact to the PR — the formal `gh pr review` verdict.
2. **Revert PR #233's signature rewording** (lines 92–93). Restores the pre-#233 single-line `Append this as the final line of the review body, verbatim: \`...\`` form. The rewording made `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` drop the signature entirely without fixing the `\n\n` issue on `openai/gpt-5.3-chat`. With the dual-post pipeline removed, the simpler "verbatim" instruction works for everyone.

Closes #235. Closes #232 (the `\n\n` literal-newline glitch is expected to resolve as a side-effect of removing the dual-post pipeline; if it persists for any model, follow-up issue).

## Why this fixes the issue

The auto-comment + comment-update tool that `track_progress: true` enables is what produced six dead `I'll analyze this and get back to you.` placeholders on PR #234, and is the most plausible source of pressure that made non-Anthropic models construct their `gh pr review -b` shell argument with literal `\n\n` instead of real newlines (Claude was juggling two posting paths and confusing the bash quoting). The action's own `action.yml` defaults `track_progress` to `false`; we'd opted into `true` back in #218 without registering the dual-post implications.

Verified the action's behaviour by reading `action.yml` and `src/modes/detector.ts` from `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1`:

- `action.yml`: *"Force tag mode with tracking comments for pull_request and issue events. Default: false."*
- `src/modes/detector.ts`: `if (context.inputs.trackProgress && isEntityContext(context)) { … force tag mode }` — false → agent mode → no auto-comment, no `update_claude_comment` injection.

## Test plan

- [x] YAML parses (`python3 -c "import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/claude-code-review.yml'))"` exits 0).
- [x] Diff scope: 1 boolean flip + 2-line revert. No other workflow content touched.
- [x] `/security-review` — no findings (no new secret refs or shell surfaces).
- [x] `/code-review` — workflow checks pass: `timeout-minutes` intact, action `@v1` pinning intact, no new env/secret refs.
- [x] Expected-failure pattern confirmed: `review` failed with workflow-tamper guard (same as #224 / #230 / #233); `check-approval` passed; `Report reviewer configuration` ran despite the failure and emitted `provider=proxy base_url=https://openrouter.ai/api sonnet=openai/gpt-5.3-chat haiku=openai/gpt-4o-mini` — `if: always()` guard intact. (Note: no auto-comment was created on this PR either, but that's because the tamper guard fires before the action reaches the comment-creation step — same on PR #233 which had `track_progress: true`. Real verification of comment suppression happens post-merge.)
- [ ] Post-merge smoke on a throwaway draft PR. Iterate `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` / `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` across the same 6 combos used on #234:
  - `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` + `openai/gpt-4o-mini` (current production; primary fix target)
  - `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` + `qwen/qwen3-coder-flash`
  - `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` on both tiers
  - `anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6` + `anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5`
  - native Anthropic (all three `vars.ANTHROPIC_*` deleted)
  - `@preset/qwen3-6-no-thinking` on both tiers

  For each iteration, two checks:
  1. `gh pr view <n> --json comments -q '[.comments[] | select(.author.login == "claude")] | length'` does **not** grow with each new commit (no new "Claude finished" comments per run).
  2. `gh pr view <n> --json reviews -q '.reviews[-1].body'` ends with the templated signature line and `grep -F '\n\n' body` returns no matches.

## Known limitation

Same `anthropics/claude-code-action@v1` workflow-tamper caveat as #224 / #230 / #233: the reviewer step on this PR will fail with *"Workflow validation failed…"* because the branch's copy of `claude-code-review.yml` differs from `main`. Expected. Real verification happens on the post-merge smoke pass described above.

items.title: Smoke: preset Qwen 3.6 retry + Minimax M2.5 comparison

content.body:
## User Story

As a repo maintainer reading `.vars.example` when picking a reviewer model, I want the listed sonnet-tier examples to be models that are verified to work cleanly with our current prompt — no `\n\n` artefact, signature preserved, proper markdown — so that any future contributor following the guidance ends up with a high-quality reviewer out of the box.

## Context

Today's cross-model smoke sweep (PRs #231, #234, #237, #238) found that the currently-documented sonnet example `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` consistently emits a literal `\n\n` before the signature line in the posted review body. This was the original bug in #232, and it survived both the prompt rewording (#233, reverted) and the `track_progress: false` switch (#235 / PR #236). The bug is model-specific — openai's SDK wrapper around `gh pr review -b "..."` passes literal backslash-n instead of a real newline.

Meanwhile, `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking` (OpenRouter preset for Minimax M2.7) was shown to satisfy every reviewer-quality property:

- No auto-comment (with `track_progress: false`).
- No `\n\n` literal in the review body.
- Signature line renders as italic markdown (`_Reviewed by proxy/<model>_`) as intended.
- Best markdown structure of any model tested: headings, bullet lists, labelled criteria assessment.
- Correct verdict routing (picked `--comment` for a draft/throwaway PR).

Full comparison from PR #238:

| Config | `\n\n`? | Signature | Markdown quality | Verdict routing |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` (current prod) | **yes** | plain | basic | correct |
| `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` | no | italic | plain | correct |
| `minimax/minimax-m2.5` (raw) | no | italic | concise | correct |
| `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-5-no-thinking` | no | italic | concise | correct |
| `minimax/minimax-m2.7` (raw) | **yes** | plain | concise | correct |
| `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking` | no | italic | **headings + bullets** | correct |

## Acceptance Criteria

**Given** a contributor reads `.vars.example` to pick a reviewer sonnet model,
**When** they look at the "Examples that work" line,
**Then** they see `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking` listed (new primary recommendation) and no longer see `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` (known-broken under our current prompt).

**Given** the production `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` variable is switched to `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking`,
**When** the next PR triggers the reviewer,
**Then** the posted review has a clean italic signature (`_Reviewed by proxy/@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking_`), no `\n\n`, no auto-comment, and the `Report reviewer configuration` step logs the new slug.

## Implementation Hint

1. Edit `.vars.example:31` — swap `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` for `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking`. Keep `qwen/qwen3-coder-plus` as the secondary example:

```diff
-# Examples that work: openai/gpt-5.3-chat, qwen/qwen3-coder-plus.
+# Examples that work: @preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking, qwen/qwen3-coder-plus.
```

No other `.vars.example` edits — the haiku-tier examples (`openai/gpt-4o-mini`, `qwen/qwen3-coder-flash`) remain; those models haven't exhibited issues and are cheap.

2. In the PR body, include a rollout step for the repo Variable: change `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` from `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` to `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking` after merge. `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_HAIKU_MODEL` stays as `openai/gpt-4o-mini`.

Closes #232 as a side-effect — the openai `\n\n` bug is resolved by removing openai from recommended usage rather than trying to patch the prompt.

## Definition of Done

- [ ] `.vars.example:31` lists `@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking` as the primary sonnet example; `openai/gpt-5.3-chat` is removed.
- [ ] No other `.vars.example` edits.
- [ ] PR body includes the repo Variable rollout step.
- [ ] Post-merge: the repo Variable `ANTHROPIC_DEFAULT_SONNET_MODEL` is updated to the preset.
- [ ] Post-rollout: next PR's review uses the preset. Confirm via `gh pr view <n> --json reviews -q '.reviews[-1].body' | tail -1` showing `_Reviewed by proxy/@preset/minimax-minimax-m2-7-no-thinking_` and via the `Report reviewer configuration` step output.
- [ ] #232 is closed.

Closes #174

## Summary

Adds a new project-scoped skill `/generate-test <page>` that scaffolds Playwright `test.fixme()` blocks for a single page's content / accessibility / visual-regression gaps recorded in `playwright/typescript/coverage-matrix.json`.

Complements `/generate-stubs` (which sweeps the whole matrix) by targeting one page at a time with richer scaffolding: imports, navigation to the page's `url`, a placeholder assertion on the page object's `heading` getter, and a TODO comment guiding the user to the real assertions.

The skill never overwrites existing files — it appends to existing spec files (with a duplicate-test guard) and only creates new files when the target does not exist. Data-driven accessibility coverage is respected: if the page is already in `PUBLIC_PAGE_CLASSES` or `AUTHENTICATED_PAGE_CLASSES`, the skill skips the accessibility scaffold and warns the user to flip the matrix value instead. `coverage-matrix.json` is intentionally not modified by the skill — values should only flip to `true` once the scaffold is replaced with real passing assertions.

## Changes

- `.claude/skills/generate-test/SKILL.md` — new skill (10 steps: resolve identifier, read matrix, identify gaps, accessibility conflict check, target-file mapping, preflight guard against overwrite/duplicate, generate scaffolds, README update for new files, no-flip rule, summary output)

content.body:
## User Story
As a repo maintainer, I want the PR test workflows to run only after the reviewer's *latest* decisional verdict is APPROVED, so that a CHANGES_REQUESTED review blocks tests until the concern is resolved.

## Context
The `check-approval` job in both `.github/workflows/bruno.yml:37-43` and `.github/workflows/playwright-typescript.yml:93-99` opens the gate if **any** historical APPROVED review exists:

```bash
COUNT=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/pulls/$PR_NUMBER/reviews" \
  --jq '[.[] | select(.state == "APPROVED")] | length')
```

GitHub's review API is append-only: a later CHANGES_REQUESTED does not retract an earlier APPROVED record. Once the reviewer has ever approved, the gate stays open forever.

The bug has existed since #130 added the gate but was latent — the pre-#223 reviewer prompt fell back to `--comment`, which doesn't produce a CHANGES_REQUESTED state. Commit `1ed1cf1` (#223) made verdicts binary (approve / request-changes), exposing the flaw.

**Evidence on PR #241:**

| Time | Actor | State |
|---|---|---|
| 11:40:45Z | claude[bot] | APPROVED |
| 11:47:25Z | claude[bot] | APPROVED |
| 12:10:48Z | claude[bot] | **CHANGES_REQUESTED** |
| 12:47:06Z | — | `check-approval` ran on new push, counted 2 stale APPROVEDs, returned `approved=true` |
| 12:47:12Z | — | Bruno tests executed (should have been skipped) |
| 12:49:04Z | claude[bot] | APPROVED (this push's verdict — arrived ~2 min *after* tests ran) |

## Acceptance Criteria
- **Given** a PR whose latest review from any user is CHANGES_REQUESTED (regardless of prior APPROVED reviews), **when** `pull_request` or `pull_request_review` triggers the workflow, **then** `check-approval` outputs `approved=false` and the test job is skipped.
- **Given** a PR whose reviewers' latest reviews include ≥1 APPROVED and 0 CHANGES_REQUESTED, **when** the workflow runs, **then** `approved=true` and tests execute.
- **Given** a PR with only COMMENTED reviews, **when** the workflow runs, **then** `approved=false`.
- **Given** a non-PR event (`push`, `schedule`), the gate continues to bypass (no regression).

## Implementation Hint
Replace the count-based check with latest-per-user state comparison in both workflows:

```bash
RESULT=$(gh api "repos/$GITHUB_REPOSITORY/pulls/$PR_NUMBER/reviews" --jq '
  [.[] | select(.state == "APPROVED" or .state == "CHANGES_REQUESTED" or .state == "DISMISSED")]
  | group_by(.user.login)
  | map(max_by(.submitted_at) | .state)
  | (any(. == "APPROVED")) and (all(. != "CHANGES_REQUESTED"))
')
if [ "$RESULT" = "true" ]; then
  echo "approved=true" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
else
  echo "approved=false" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
fi
```

COMMENTED reviews are deliberately excluded so a follow-up comment after an APPROVED review doesn't invalidate the approval.

## Definition of Done
- [ ] `check-approval` in `bruno.yml` and `playwright-typescript.yml` uses latest-per-user decisional review state instead of historical APPROVED count
- [ ] Verified against PR #241: jq expression returns `false` at 12:47:06Z snapshot and `true` at current HEAD
- [ ] On a test PR where Claude issues CHANGES_REQUESTED, subsequent `pull_request` events skip the test job
- [ ] On a test PR where Claude approves, tests run as before
- [ ] `push` to `main` and `workflow_dispatch` still bypass the gate

content.body:
Closes #242

## Summary
Replaces the count-based PR approval check in `bruno.yml` and `playwright-typescript.yml` with latest-per-user decisional-review logic, so a CHANGES_REQUESTED review actually blocks the test job (the previous logic counted every historical APPROVED, including ones superseded by later CHANGES_REQUESTED).

The fix:
- Filters reviews to APPROVED / CHANGES_REQUESTED / DISMISSED (COMMENTED ignored)
- Groups by `user.login`, takes each user's latest by `submitted_at`
- Opens the gate only if ≥1 user's latest is APPROVED and no user's latest is CHANGES_REQUESTED
- Uses `gh api --paginate ... | jq` so the group_by sees the full review history (`gh --jq` runs per-page and would break the grouping)

## Test plan
- [x] jq verified against PR #241's full review history → returns `true` (current HEAD has APPROVED as the latest review)
- [x] jq verified against PR #241 snapshot at 12:47:06Z (the buggy run) → returns `false` (CHANGES_REQUESTED was the latest review at that moment)
- [x] Synthetic case: only COMMENTED reviews → `false`
- [x] Synthetic case: empty reviews → `false`
- [x] Synthetic case: two approvers + one CHANGES_REQUESTED from a third user → `false`
- [x] Synthetic case: same user APPROVED then COMMENTED → `true`
- [x] Synthetic case: same user APPROVED then DISMISSED → `false`
- [x] `gh api --paginate ... | jq 'length'` returns the full count (7 on PR #241, vs `--jq 'length'` which returns per-page counts)
- [x] `--paginate` with `per_page=100` requires only one API call for typical PRs
- [x] **Live:** this PR's first `pull_request` run (13:20:18Z) found 0 reviews → `check-approval` returned false → Bruno `test` job **SKIPPED** (confirms the gate is now properly closed by default — previously it would have run with stale APPROVEDs from any prior PR pattern)
- [x] **Live:** after Claude approved at 13:21:11Z, the `pull_request_review` re-trigger ran `check-approval` → returned true → Bruno `test` job **SUCCESS**
- [ ] Live: CHANGES_REQUESTED scenario isn't naturally exercised on this PR — verified by jq scenario above plus historical PR #241 snapshot
